title: vatica patentinervia
intro: Vatica patentinervia, also known as the penai or bintangor tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae that is native to Borneo. The medicinal properties of Vatica patentinervia are attributed to its bark, which contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Vatica patentinervia include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Vatica patentinervia can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
